message:
feat(async-core): expand fcp-async-core into owned facade over tokio with asupersync timeout

Transform fcp-async-core from thin re-exports of tokio types into a
proper owned facade layer that wraps tokio primitives behind crate-local
types. This is the foundational step toward making the runtime swappable.

Key changes:
- `select!` and `task_local!` macros now delegate through
  `$crate::__private::tokio` instead of bare `pub use tokio::*`
- New `runtime` module with owned `Builder` and `Runtime` structs that
  wrap `tokio::runtime::Builder`/`Runtime` with identical API surface
- New `time` module with owned `Sleep` struct (wraps
  `Pin<Box<tokio::time::Sleep>>` implementing `Future`) and `Interval`
  struct; `timeout()` now delegates to `asupersync::time::timeout()`
  for improved cancellation semantics
- New `channel` module with sub-modules `broadcast`, `mpsc`, `oneshot`,
  `watch` — each exports type aliases plus factory functions
- `sync` module: changed from `pub use` to `type` aliases for `Mutex`,
  `RwLock`, `Semaphore`, `OwnedSemaphorePermit`
- `task` module: owned `spawn()` and `yield_now()` functions
- `io`, `process`, `net` modules: changed from re-exports to type aliases
- `TaskGroup::shutdown`: now uses per-task remaining deadline via
  `Instant` arithmetic instead of same timeout for every task
- Add `asupersync` path dependency for timeout and future primitives
- 3 new tests: block_on_sync outside runtime, IO driver support,
  timeout elapsed-to-async error mapping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
